name: Indian Government (Ministry of Steel & Commerce)
description: The Indian central government acting through the Ministry of Steel and Ministry of Commerce & Industry. Responsible for trade policy, industrial policy, and protecting domestic industry while maintaining international trade relations.
objectives: Protect domestic steel industry and manufacturing jobs
Maintain stable steel prices for downstream industries (auto, construction, infra)
Strengthen India's position in global trade negotiations
Promote 'Make in India' and Atmanirbhar Bharat (self-reliance) in steel
constraints: WTO commitments — cannot impose arbitrary tariffs without justification
Diplomatic relationships — retaliatory tariffs risk escalation
Downstream industry impact — high steel prices hurt auto, construction sectors
Political economy — must balance producer and consumer interests
Fiscal constraints — subsidies need budgetary allocation

trade_policy_current.anti_dumping_duties: Active on Chinese, Korean, Japanese steel products
trade_policy_current.export_incentives: MEIS/RoDTEP applicable on select steel products
trade_policy_current.quality_control_orders: BIS certification mandatory for 145+ steel products

us_india_trade_context.india_retaliatory_tariffs: Imposed counter-tariffs on select US goods in 2019

policy_tools_available: Issue quality control orders to restrict imports
Provide export subsidies or tax benefits
